hero.title:
Where Eastern Heritage
Meets the World
hero.subtitle: Preserving Heritage. Inspiring Dialogue. Bridging Cultures.

items.title: Chinese Calligraphy & Painting
items.description: Historic scrolls and ink paintings that embody the depth of Chinese literary and visual culture across the centuries.

items.title: Animal Painting · Tiger
items.description: Expressive splash-ink animal paintings led by Zhang Jinsheng, celebrating one of Chinese art's most powerful symbolic traditions.

items.title: Landscape Ink Art
items.description: Masterworks of Chinese landscape painting, where brushwork and mist evoke the Eastern philosophy of harmony between humanity and nature.

items.title: Asian Traditional Arts
items.description: Ceramics, bronzeware, silk embroidery and heritage objects spanning Asia's great civilizations, presented in contemporary context.

items.title: Intangible Heritage
items.description: Preserving and promoting traditional crafts, folk arts and living cultural practices as vital threads of human civilization.

items.title: Contemporary Eastern Art
items.description: Works by contemporary artists who engage Eastern aesthetics to address modern questions, bridging tradition and innovation.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: MOECA
about.heading: A federally incorporated non-profit institution dedicated to Eastern cultures and arts
about.body: Based in Montréal and serving an international audience, MOECA fosters intercultural understanding by presenting the artistic, historical, and intellectual heritage of Eastern civilizations. Rooted in the rich traditions of Chinese culture while embracing the diversity of Asia.

artist.title: Contemporary Ink Artist
artist.timeline_subtitle: A lifetime dedicated to Eastern ink art and cultural exchange.
artist.about_text: Zhang Jinsheng's art transcends mere representation — each tiger is a portrait of human emotion, rendered through bold, uninhibited brushwork.
artist.about_heading: Pioneering the splash-ink tiger — where raw energy meets classical tradition.
artist.about_body: Inspired by Zhang Daqian's revolutionary splash-ink technique, Zhang Jinsheng developed his own expressive vocabulary. His tigers embody courage, solitude, and the full spectrum of human feeling, rendered with ink that flows as freely as emotion itself. Over decades of practice, he has used the tiger as a mirror to reflect the human condition — each work a silent dialogue with the viewer, transcending language and culture to reach the soul.

brief.school: Dafeng Tang — lineage of Zhang Daqian

artist.bio: A second-generation disciple of the legendary Dafeng Tang school — the artistic lineage of Zhang Daqian and Zhang Shanzi — Zhang Jinsheng has dedicated over fifty years to the art of Chinese ink painting. Renowned for his expressive splash-ink tigers, he is widely acclaimed as the foremost tiger painter of the Central Plains.

timeline.event: Studied under Li Baoduo, joined Dafeng Tang lineage
Solo exhibition: One Hundred Tigers, Zhengzhou
Published monograph Zhang Jinsheng Paints Tigers
Exhibition in Taipei; donated documents to National Palace Museum
Shanghai exhibition attended by Zhang Xinqing, daughter of Zhang Daqian
Opened personal art gallery in Shanghai, held a calligraphy and painting exhibition to wide acclaim from collectors and media

items.title: Collection
items.desc: Systematically acquiring Eastern cultural artworks of scholarly and historical significance, preserving the finest expressions of human civilization for future generations.

items.title: Research
items.desc: Conducting in-depth research into the history, aesthetics and cultural meaning of Eastern art, advancing academic knowledge and its dissemination.

items.title: Exhibition
items.desc: Curating high-calibre exhibitions that present the artistic beauty of Eastern civilization to international audiences, fostering cross-cultural visual dialogue.

items.title: Education
items.desc: Developing diverse public education programmes that help audiences of all ages and backgrounds engage with the spiritual values and historical roots of Eastern culture.

items.title: International Exchange
items.desc: Building partnerships with museums, academic institutions and artists worldwide to deepen dialogue and mutual inspiration between Eastern and Western civilizations.

items.content: MOECA's exhibitions are a remarkable window into Eastern civilization. The curation is thoughtful, the artworks breathtaking. An essential cultural institution for Montréal.
items.author: Sophie Tremblay

items.content: The ink tiger paintings by Zhang Jinsheng are unlike anything I have seen before. Raw power and ancient tradition meet in a way that is truly unforgettable.
items.author: Michael Chen

items.content: A beautiful and inspiring space. MOECA bridges Eastern and Western cultures with grace and depth. The collection of works by Zhang Jinsheng is extraordinary.
items.author: David Laurent

items.content: As a Chinese-Canadian, MOECA feels like home. It celebrates our cultural heritage with dignity and artistic excellence. I return every season.
items.author: Wang Jianguo

items.content: I came in knowing nothing about Chinese ink painting and left completely captivated. The guides are knowledgeable and the atmosphere is both serene and powerful.
items.author: Emma Beauchamp

team.title: Resident Artists
team.banner_heading: MOECA brings together distinguished artists from China and across Asia
team.banner_body: United by a shared dedication to Eastern art and cultural heritage, our resident artists use ink and brush to bridge civilizations and inspire dialogue across borders.

history.block1_desc: The Museum is officially incorporated under the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act, dedicated to the preservation, research, exhibition and promotion of Eastern cultures and arts.
history.block2_desc: The Museum's first exhibition features splash-ink paintings and calligraphy by Zhang Jinsheng, a master of the Dafeng Tang lineage, opening a new chapter in East-West cultural dialogue.
history.block3_desc: MOECA continues to build partnerships with museums, academic institutions and artists worldwide, bringing Eastern civilization to the global stage.

about_hero.subtitle: Our History
about_hero.p1: MOECA — Canadian Museum of Eastern Culture and Art — is a federally incorporated non-profit cultural institution established in Montréal, Canada. Founded to bridge Eastern and Western civilizations, the Museum is dedicated to the preservation, research, and promotion of Eastern cultures and arts.
about_hero.p2: Rooted in the rich traditions of Chinese culture while embracing the full diversity of Asia, the Museum serves as a platform for dialogue, learning, and shared understanding across cultures and generations.

why_choose.title: Why Choose MOECA?
items: Authentic Dafeng Tang lineage — deep artistic heritage
Based in Montréal, serving the global Chinese community
A leading platform for East-West cultural exchange
